summary: Adjacent same-name Swarm-trait enemies acting as one mass.
description: Enemies with the Swarm trait may join adjacent enemies with the same name to form a Swarm. A Swarm has HP equal to its square count × 10, targets only adjacent squares, and ignores elevation. It moves by rearranging one component square per point of Speed. Attacks targeting any square hit the whole Swarm unless they have Breaker. Push and forced movement affect only the targeted square(s).
A minion or unit that has joined a Swarm and is treated as part of that single mass.

summary: Moving away from an adjacent unit triggers a 1D6 attack before movement.
description: When adjacent units move away from one another, they trigger Provoke. The moving unit suffers a 1D6 attack against it before they move. Swarms only Provoke when their last square moves away from another unit.

summary: Tag for deployed structures and stationary battlefield assets.
description: Fortifications are enemy units representing structures, engines, or other stationary battlefield assets. They follow their listed stats and special rules rather than standard demon movement.

summary: Move over terrain unimpeded with +1 Elevation and no Provoke.
description: Flying units move over all types of terrain unimpeded, are treated as having +1 Elevation, and do not Provoke.

summary: Weaker enemy variety, often eligible for Swarms.
description: Minions are lower-tier enemies. Many minions have the Swarm trait and can combine into Swarms with other same-name minions.

summary: A faction's one-line identity and a numeric gate (starts at 5, minimum 1).
description: Crowns are a single sentence that conveys a Faction's missions, goals, aesthetics, values, or aspirations. Crowns also have a value beginning at 5, lowered by one each time the Faction loses two Quality points (and raised when it regains two). This value gates special Demons and abilities. Crowns cannot fall below 1 and do not count toward defeating a faction. Units with a Crown value can only be deployed when their faction's Crown is at or below that value.

summary: Four abstract measures of a Faction's capacity to act in the world.
description: Qualities represent wealth, land, military force, research, brutality, subterfuge, and similar capacity as dots from 1–5 (0 when stripped). They are attached to buildings and locations the faction controls; losing or gaining those sites changes Qualities. Lowering at least two Qualities to zero unlocks Final Assault against that Demon Prince.

summary: Martial power: troops, logistics, fortifications, and projected might.
description: Force measures a Faction's military strength. One dot is specialized small-squad tactics with little depth; five dots is a terrifying army of experienced, well-equipped troops.

summary: Spies, tricks, misdirection, betrayals, and underhand tactics.
description: Subterfuge measures a Faction's capacity for covert action. Low investment means clumsy or rare intrigue; high investment means deep spy networks and constant misdirection.

summary: Land quality, development, defenses, and exploitation.
description: Territory measures how much and how well a Faction holds and develops land. Low Territory is sparse or poorly held ground; high Territory is densely controlled, fortified, and exploited.

summary: Wealth, artifacts, inventions, and converting riches into war resources.
description: Assets measure a Faction's material and magical wealth. Low Assets means scarce reserves; high Assets means vast stores that can be poured into the war effort.

summary: Ascended warriors who descend into Hell under Syncrasis.
description: HELLPIERCERS are the player characters: humans remade by Syncrasis to fight in Hell with nouswork armor, legendary arms, and TACCOM tactics.
